speaker: Hannah
prompt_context: At Shiloh, while deeply distressed, a barren woman prayed and vowed to dedicate a son to the Lord if one were given to her.
question: Who prayed at Shiloh and asked God to remember her in her distress?

speaker: Eli
quote_text: How long will you make a drunken spectacle?
prompt_context: While watching a woman pray silently at the sanctuary, the priest misunderstood what he saw and rebuked her.
question: Who wrongly accused Hannah of being drunk while she prayed silently?

speaker: Hannah
quote_text: I have been pouring out my soul
prompt_context: After being mistaken for a drunk woman, the worshiper explained her grief and earnest prayer to the priest.
question: Who told Eli that she had been pouring out her soul before the Lord?

speaker: Eli
prompt_context: After hearing Hannah's explanation, the priest sent her away with a blessing.
question: Who told Hannah to go in peace and asked that God grant her petition?

speaker: Hannah
prompt_context: When returning to Shiloh with the boy she had received, the mother reminded Eli of her earlier prayer and vow.
question: Who told Eli, "For this child I prayed," when bringing the boy to the sanctuary?

speaker: Man of God
quote_text: Those who honor me I will honor
prompt_context: A prophetic word came against Eli's house because his sons had despised the Lord's offerings and Eli had not restrained them.
question: Who delivered the prophetic word to Eli that those who honor the Lord would be honored?

speaker: Samuel
quote_text: Speak, for your servant is listening
prompt_context: As a boy in the temple, after Eli told him how to answer God's call.
question: Who answered God's call by saying he was listening?

speaker: God
quote_text: I am about to do something in Israel
prompt_context: When Samuel finally answered correctly, the Lord revealed an alarming judgment about Eli's household.
question: Who told Samuel that he was about to do something in Israel that would make both ears of everyone who heard it tingle?

speaker: Eli
quote_text: What was it that he told you?
prompt_context: After Samuel received the night vision, the old priest pressed the boy to reveal the whole message.
question: Who urged Samuel not to hide any part of the message he had received from the Lord?

speaker: Elders of Israel
prompt_context: After Israel was defeated by the Philistines, leaders proposed bringing the ark from Shiloh into battle.
question: Who proposed bringing the ark of the covenant from Shiloh after Israel was defeated by the Philistines?

speaker: Philistines
prompt_context: When they heard Israel's shout over the arrival of the ark, the Philistines panicked and tried to steel themselves for battle.
question: Who cried out in fear, asking who could deliver them from these mighty gods?

speaker: Samuel
prompt_context: As Israel turned from foreign gods, the prophet called the people to wholehearted repentance and exclusive devotion.
question: Who told Israel to return to the Lord with all their heart and put away foreign gods?

speaker: Elders of Israel
prompt_context: When Samuel had grown old and his sons did not walk in his ways, the nation's elders came to him with a request.
question: Who asked Samuel to appoint them a king like the nations?

speaker: God
prompt_context: After Samuel was displeased by the demand for a king, the Lord clarified what Israel's request really meant.
question: Who told Samuel that the people had not rejected him but had rejected God from reigning over them?

speaker: Samuel
quote_text: These will be the ways of the king
prompt_context: Before Israel insisted on a monarchy, Samuel warned them what a king would take from them.
question: Who warned Israel about the costly demands a human king would place on them?

speaker: People of Israel
quote_text: No, but we will have a king
prompt_context: Despite Samuel's warning, the nation persisted in wanting a ruler like the surrounding peoples.
question: Who insisted that they still wanted a king to judge them and go out before them in battle?

speaker: God
prompt_context: Before Saul arrived, the Lord told Samuel that the man from Benjamin would become leader over his people.
question: Who told Samuel to anoint the Benjamite who would come the next day?

speaker: Samuel
prompt_context: When Saul came searching for donkeys, the prophet revealed his own identity and hinted at greater things.
question: Who told Saul, "I am the seer," before inviting him to the high place?

speaker: Saul
prompt_context: When Samuel honored him unexpectedly, Saul answered with surprise about his tribe and family.
question: Who responded that he came from the smallest tribe and a humble family in Benjamin?

speaker: Samuel
quote_text: Has not the Lord anointed you?
prompt_context: After taking a flask of oil and kissing Saul, the prophet explained the significance of what had happened.
question: Who told Saul that the Lord had anointed him ruler over his people?

speaker: Samuel
quote_text: Go down before me to Gilgal
prompt_context: As part of the signs confirming Saul's call, Samuel gave instructions about waiting for him at a future gathering.
question: Who instructed Saul to go ahead to Gilgal and wait seven days?

speaker: Saul
quote_text: He told us plainly about the donkeys
prompt_context: When questioned by his uncle after meeting Samuel, Saul concealed the matter of kingship.
question: Who told his uncle only that Samuel had spoken about the lost donkeys?

speaker: Samuel
quote_text: See him whom the Lord has chosen
prompt_context: At Mizpah, after Saul was brought forward before the assembled tribes, the prophet presented him publicly.
question: Who said to the people, "See him whom the Lord has chosen"?

speaker: Worthless Men
quote_text: How can this man save us?
prompt_context: After Saul was publicly chosen, some opposed him and despised his kingship.
question: Who said, "How can this man save us?" after Saul was chosen?

speaker: Nahash the Ammonite
quote_text: I will gouge out the right eye
prompt_context: When Jabesh-gilead asked for a treaty, the Ammonite king answered with a humiliating condition.
question: Who threatened to gouge out the right eye of the men of Jabesh-gilead?

speaker: Saul
quote_text: No one shall be put to death today
prompt_context: After the rescue of Jabesh-gilead, Saul refused to retaliate against earlier opponents.
question: Who said that no one would be put to death that day because the Lord had worked salvation in Israel?

speaker: Samuel
quote_text: Here I am; testify against me
prompt_context: At Saul's renewal of kingship, the aging judge publicly invited Israel to speak if he had wronged anyone.
question: Who challenged Israel to testify if he had taken anyone's ox, donkey, or bribe?

speaker: People of Israel
prompt_context: After thunder and rain exposed their sin in asking for a king, the people pleaded with the prophet for intercession.
question: Who begged Samuel to pray that they might not die because they had added asking for a king to their sins?

speaker: Samuel
quote_text: Far be it from me that I should sin
prompt_context: In response to Israel's plea, Samuel promised continued prayer and faithful instruction.
question: Who said it would be a sin against the Lord for him to cease praying for Israel?

speaker: Jonathan
prompt_context: Planning a surprise attack with only his armor-bearer, Jonathan expressed confidence that the Lord could save by many or by few.
question: Who said that nothing could hinder the Lord from saving by many or by few?

speaker: Jonathan's Armor-bearer
quote_text: Do all that is in your heart
prompt_context: After Jonathan proposed the daring attack, his young companion answered with loyalty.
question: Who told Jonathan to do all that was in his heart because he was with him entirely?

speaker: Samuel
prompt_context: When Saul offered the sacrifice before Samuel arrived, the prophet rebuked him for not keeping the Lord's command.
question: Who told Saul that he had done foolishly and that his kingdom would not continue?

speaker: Saul
quote_text: Cursed be the man who eats food
prompt_context: During the pursuit of the Philistines, Saul bound the people with a rash oath that weakened them.
question: Who placed Israel under an oath forbidding food before evening?

speaker: Jonathan
quote_text: My father has troubled the land
prompt_context: Unaware of Saul's oath, Jonathan tasted honey and then criticized the restriction after hearing about it.
question: Who said that his father had troubled the land after tasting honey?

speaker: Samuel
prompt_context: Before Saul's campaign against Amalek, the prophet delivered a command of total judgment from the Lord.
question: Who instructed Saul to strike Amalek and devote all they had to destruction?

speaker: Saul
quote_text: I have carried out the command
prompt_context: After the Amalekite campaign, Saul greeted Samuel with confidence before the prophet exposed his disobedience.
question: Who told Samuel that he had carried out the Lord's command, despite sparing what should have been destroyed?

speaker: Samuel
prompt_context: After Saul claimed obedience, the prophet pointed to the obvious evidence of spared livestock.
question: Who asked Saul about the bleating of sheep and lowing of cattle he could hear?

speaker: Samuel
quote_text: To obey is better than sacrifice
prompt_context: When Saul defended his actions with religious language, Samuel declared the priority of obedience over ritual.
question: Who told Saul that obedience was better than sacrifice and listening than the fat of rams?

speaker: Saul
prompt_context: Confronted by Samuel after the Amalekite campaign, Saul admitted wrongdoing but emphasized fear of the people.
question: Who confessed that he had sinned because he feared the people and obeyed their voice?

speaker: God
quote_text: How long will you grieve over Saul?
prompt_context: After Saul's rejection, the Lord redirected Samuel from mourning toward the selection of a new king in Bethlehem.
question: Who asked Samuel how long he would grieve over Saul and sent him to Jesse?

speaker: God
quote_text: The Lord does not see as mortals see
prompt_context: When Samuel was impressed by Jesse's older son, the Lord corrected the prophet's judgment.
question: Who told Samuel that people look on the outward appearance, but the Lord looks on the heart?

speaker: Saul's Servants
prompt_context: When an evil spirit troubled Saul, his servants proposed a skilled musician to soothe him.
question: Who suggested to Saul that someone skillful with the lyre be found for him?

speaker: Goliath
prompt_context: Standing between the armies, the Philistine champion challenged Israel to settle the conflict by single combat.
question: Who challenged Israel to send out one man to fight him?

speaker: David
prompt_context: Hearing the giant's defiance for the first time, the young shepherd reacted with zeal for God's honor.
question: Who asked who the uncircumcised Philistine was that he should defy the armies of the living God?

speaker: David
prompt_context: Brought before Saul, the young man volunteered to face the Philistine despite everyone else's fear.
question: Who told Saul that no one's heart should fail because of Goliath?

speaker: Saul
prompt_context: When David offered to fight the giant, Saul answered by pointing to the youth's inexperience.
question: Who told David that he was not able to go against the Philistine because he was only a youth?

speaker: David
prompt_context: Explaining why he was confident against Goliath, David recalled rescue from lion and bear alike.
question: Who said that the Lord who delivered him from lion and bear would deliver him from the Philistine?

speaker: David
quote_text: I come to you in the name of the Lord
prompt_context: Standing before the giant, David contrasted Goliath's weapons with his own reliance on the Lord.
question: Who told Goliath that he came in the name of the Lord of hosts?

speaker: Saul
quote_text: Whose son are you, young man?
prompt_context: After the defeat of Goliath, the king questioned the victor about his family background.
question: Who asked David whose son he was after the battle with Goliath?

speaker: Women of Israel
prompt_context: As the armies returned, singing women celebrated victories in a way that inflamed the king's jealousy.
question: Who sang that Saul had slain his thousands, and David his ten thousands?

speaker: Jonathan
prompt_context: Trying to protect his friend, Jonathan spoke favorably to Saul and reminded him of David's service and victory.
question: Who pleaded with Saul not to sin against David, who had risked his life against the Philistine?

speaker: Michal
prompt_context: After helping David escape from Saul, Michal gave an explanation when her father confronted her.
question: Who told Saul that David had said, “Let me go,” and had threatened her?

speaker: Jonathan
quote_text: Whatever you say, I will do for you
prompt_context: When David shared his fear of Saul's intentions, Jonathan responded with loyalty and willingness to help.
question: Who told David that he would do whatever David asked of him?

speaker: David
quote_text: There is but a step between me and death
prompt_context: Trying to convince Jonathan of the danger he faced from Saul, David described his peril bluntly.
question: Who said there was only a step between him and death because of Saul?

speaker: Saul
quote_text: As long as the son of Jesse lives
prompt_context: At the new moon table, Saul's anger against Jonathan erupted when David's absence exposed their loyalty conflict.
question: Who told Jonathan that as long as David lived, neither Jonathan nor his kingdom would be established?

speaker: Ahimelech
prompt_context: When David arrived at Nob without his usual company, the priest questioned the unusual circumstances.
question: Who asked David why he was alone and no one was with him at Nob?

speaker: Doeg the Edomite
quote_text: I saw the son of Jesse
prompt_context: When Saul accused his servants of conspiracy, one court official reported David's visit to the priests.
question: Who told Saul that he had seen David come to Ahimelech at Nob?

speaker: Abiathar
prompt_context: After the massacre at Nob, one surviving priest fled to David and reported what had happened.
question: Who escaped from the house of Ahimelech and told David that Saul had killed the priests of the Lord?

speaker: David
quote_text: Shall I go and attack these Philistines?
prompt_context: When he heard that Keilah was being raided, David first sought divine guidance before acting.
question: Who asked the Lord whether he should go and strike the Philistines at Keilah?

speaker: Jonathan
prompt_context: In the wilderness, Jonathan strengthened David's hand in God and affirmed his future kingship.
question: Who told David not to be afraid because Saul would not find him and he would be king?

speaker: David
quote_text: I will not raise my hand
prompt_context: After sparing Saul in the cave, David called out to explain why he had not killed the king when he had the chance.
question: Who told Saul that he would not raise his hand against the Lord's anointed?

speaker: Saul
quote_text: You are more righteous than I
prompt_context: After hearing David's appeal from the cave, Saul was moved and admitted David's better conduct.
question: Who told David that he was more righteous than himself because David had repaid good for evil?

speaker: Nabal
prompt_context: When David's men requested provisions after protecting Nabal's shepherds, Nabal answered with insult and contempt.
question: Who scornfully asked, "Who is David?" when his servants brought a request for food?

speaker: Abigail
prompt_context: Meeting David with gifts, Abigail appealed to him not to avenge himself in bloodshed over Nabal's offense.
question: Who asked David to forgive the trespass and not to have bloodguilt on his conscience?

speaker: David
prompt_context: After Abigail's wise intervention, David recognized that the Lord had used her to keep him from bloodshed.
question: Who praised Abigail's discretion for restraining him from vengeance?

speaker: Abishai
quote_text: God has given your enemy into your hand
prompt_context: When David and Abishai reached Saul sleeping in the camp, Abishai urged immediate action.
question: Who told David that God had given Saul into his hand and offered to pin him to the earth with the spear?

speaker: David
quote_text: Who can raise a hand against the Lord's anointed?
prompt_context: Even with Saul helpless before him, David refused to permit the king's death by his companion's hand.
question: Who said that no one could raise a hand against the Lord's anointed and remain guiltless?

speaker: Saul
prompt_context: After David again spared him and called out from a safe distance, Saul responded with confession and blessing.
question: Who confessed, "I have sinned," after David spared his life in the camp?

speaker: Achish
quote_text: Very well, I will make you my bodyguard
prompt_context: When David promised to accompany the Philistines, the Philistine king responded with trust and promotion.
question: Who told David that he would make him his bodyguard for life?

speaker: Saul
quote_text: Bring up for me the one I name
prompt_context: Desperate before battle and abandoned by the usual means of guidance, Saul went in disguise to consult a medium.
question: Who told the woman at Endor to bring up for him the one he named?

speaker: Samuel
prompt_context: After being summoned through the medium, Samuel announced that the Lord had turned from Saul and that disaster was imminent.
question: Who asked Saul why he consulted him, since the Lord had become Saul's enemy?

speaker: Philistine Commanders
prompt_context: As the Philistine army prepared for battle, the commanders objected to David marching with them.
question: Who demanded that David be sent back instead of going into battle with the Philistines?

speaker: God
prompt_context: After Ziklag was burned and the people were carried off, David inquired of the Lord about chasing the raiders.
question: Who told David to pursue the raiding party because he would surely overtake them and rescue the captives?

speaker: Saul
prompt_context: Wounded in the final battle against the Philistines, Saul feared abuse if he fell alive into enemy hands.
question: Who told his armor-bearer to draw his sword and kill him on Mount Gilboa?

speaker: David
prompt_context: After recovering everything from the Amalekite raiders, some men wanted to deny a share of the spoil to those who had stayed behind.
question: Who said the men who stayed with the supplies should share alike with those who went into battle?
